# RustySNES — PPU (5C77 + 5C78) Supplemental Research

**Generated:** 2026-06-24 · Immutable supplement to `research-report.md`. Do not edit; new findings → new dated file.

Covers the two-chip PPU register/timeline model, BG modes, OAM/sprites, CGRAM/VRAM, color math,
windows, DMA/HDMA registers, and frame structure. Every nontrivial claim is cited.

---

## 1. PPU1 (5C77) vs PPU2 (5C78) — chip split

The PPU is two chips on the CPU's **B-bus** ($2100–$21FF), sharing a 16-bit data bus but with
separate address buses.

- **PPU1 = 5C77:** OAM/sprites + the background/sprite rendering pipeline, including the **Mode 7
  multiply** result. `STAT77 $213E` = PPU1 version + sprite over/time flags; the Mode 7 multiply
  result (MPYL/M/H, $2134–6) is read from PPU1. ([Fullsnes](https://problemkaputt.de/fullsnes.htm))
- **PPU2 = 5C78:** CGRAM (palette) access, color math/output, and the timing/status flags (H/V
  counters, VBlank/HBlank status, the latch). `STAT78 $213F` = PPU2 version + NTSC/PAL +
  counter-latch flag. ([Fullsnes](https://problemkaputt.de/fullsnes.htm))

This split maps cleanly onto two sub-modules sharing the B-bus in `rustysnes-ppu`.

## 2. Background modes 0–7

| Mode | BG1 | BG2 | BG3 | BG4 | Layers | Special |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | 2bpp | 2bpp | 2bpp | 2bpp | 4 | separate palette region per BG |
| 1 | 4bpp | 4bpp | 2bpp | — | 3 | BG3 priority-bit selectable (BGMODE bit 3) |
| 2 | 4bpp | 4bpp | (OPT) | — | 2 | offset-per-tile (BG3 = offset data) |
| 3 | 8bpp | 4bpp | — | — | 2 | high-color |
| 4 | 8bpp | 2bpp | (OPT) | — | 2 | offset-per-tile |
| 5 | 4bpp | 2bpp | — | — | 2 | forced 512-px hi-res (16×8 tiles) |
| 6 | 4bpp | — | (OPT) | — | 1 | hi-res + offset-per-tile |
| 7 | 8bpp | — | — | — | 1 | affine rotation/scaling; +EXTBG → BG2 |

Sources: [SNESdev Backgrounds](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/Backgrounds), [Fullsnes](https://problemkaputt.de/fullsnes.htm).

- **BGMODE $2105:** bits 0–2 = mode, bit 3 = BG3 priority (Mode 1), bits 4–7 = per-BG tile size
  (8×8 vs 16×16). ([SNESdev PPU registers](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/PPU_registers))
- **Offset-per-tile** in Modes 2/4/6 only (a BG layer encodes per-column scroll). **Hi-res Modes
  5/6** force 512-px horizontal (16×8 tile when not interlaced). ([SNESdev Backgrounds](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/Backgrounds))

### Mode 7 affine

Single **128×128-tile** map (256 distinct 8×8 tiles), 8bpp, affine 2×2 matrix.

- **Matrix M7A $211B / M7B $211C / M7C $211D / M7D $211E** — each written twice (low then high),
  **16-bit signed 1/256 fixed-point** (8.8). Typical: M7A=+cos·SX, M7B=+sin·SX, M7C=−sin·SY,
  M7D=+cos·SY. ([SNESdev PPU registers](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/PPU_registers), [Fullsnes](https://problemkaputt.de/fullsnes.htm))
- **Center M7X $211F / M7Y $2120** — 13-bit signed, written twice. **M7SEL $211A** = wrap/flip.
- **Hardware multiply reuse:** M7A × (M7B high byte) → signed 24-bit at **MPYL/M/H $2134–$2136**
  (general-purpose multiplier, no delay). ([SNESdev PPU registers](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/PPU_registers))
- **EXTBG (SETINI $2133 bit 6):** turns Mode 7's high color bit into a per-pixel priority bit
  (BG1 8bpp + BG2 7bpp). ([Fullsnes](https://problemkaputt.de/fullsnes.htm))

## 3. OAM / sprite model

- **128 sprites**, **OAM = 544 bytes** = low table 512 + high table 32. ([SNESdev Sprites](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/Sprites), [Fullsnes](https://problemkaputt.de/fullsnes.htm))
- **Low table (4 B/sprite):** byte0 X-low, byte1 Y, byte2 tile-low, byte3 `VHPP CCCt` = name high
  bit, 3-bit palette, 2-bit priority, V/H flip. **High table (2 b/sprite):** X high bit +
  size-toggle. ([SNESdev Sprites](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/Sprites))
- **OBSEL $2101** `SSS NN bBB`: name base, name select, and the size pair (0–7): 8/16, 8/32,
  8/64, 16/32, 16/64, 32/64, + two rectangular pairs. ([SNESdev PPU registers](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/PPU_registers), [Fullsnes](https://problemkaputt.de/fullsnes.htm))
- **Per-scanline limits:** **Range Over = 32 sprites/line** (STAT77 bit 6); **Time Over = 34
  sprite-tiles/slivers/line** (STAT77 bit 7); both reset at end of VBlank. **Lower OAM index = on
  top**; tile fetch is reverse-order so **low-index sprites drop first** on overflow. ([SNESdev Sprites](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/Sprites))

## 4. Dot-clock timeline & H/V counters

- 1 dot = **4 master clocks** nominal; normal line = **1364 clocks = 341 dots** (336×4 + 4×5, or
  SNESdev's "340 dots, dots 323/327 = 6 clocks"). Short = 1360 (NTSC non-interlace V=240 alt
  frames); long = 1368 (PAL interlace field=1 V=311). ([SNESdev Timing](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/Timing), [Super Famicom Dev](https://wiki.superfamicom.org/timing))
- **262/312 lines (NTSC/PAL)** non-interlace, +1 interlaced. Active output dots 22–277 on lines
  1–224 (or 1–239 overscan). **VBlank** at V=225 (or V=240 overscan). **HBlank** H=274→H=1.
  ([Super Famicom Dev — Timing](https://wiki.superfamicom.org/timing))
- **H/V latch:** read **SLHV $2137** latches H/V; **OPHCT $213C / OPVCT $213D** read twice for the
  9-bit values; **STAT78 $213F** read clears latch + resets toggle. ([SNESdev PPU registers](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/PPU_registers))

## 5. DMA & HDMA registers

8 channels (0–7), registers `$43n0–$43nA`. DMA reads one bus / writes the other; **cannot cross a
bank**.

- **$43n0 DMAPn:** bit7 direction, bit6 HDMA indirect, bits4–3 A-step, bits2–0 transfer pattern.
  **$43n1 BBADn:** B-bus target ($21xx). **$43n2–4 A1Tn:** 24-bit A-bus source. **$43n5–7 DASn:**
  byte count / indirect addr. **$43n8–9 A2An:** HDMA table addr. **$43nA NLTRn:** line counter +
  repeat. ([SNESdev DMA registers](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/DMA_registers))
- **Transfer patterns 0–7:** 0 = 1 byte; 1 = 2 (B, B+1); 2 = 2 (B, B); 3 = 4 (B,B,B+1,B+1);
  4 = 4 (B..B+3); etc. ([SNESdev DMA registers](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/DMA_registers))
- **MDMAEN $420B** starts GP-DMA (CPU halts until done; fires mid following-instruction).
  **HDMAEN $420C** enables HDMA. ([SNESdev DMA registers](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/DMA_registers))
- **Cycle costs:** GP-DMA 8 clk/byte + 8/channel + 12–24 align. HDMA ~18 overhead +8/direct
  channel/line +8/byte; indirect 24/channel (16 pointer load); worst case ~466 clk/line; HDMA
  fires ~H=$116 and **preempts GP-DMA**. ([Super Famicom Dev — DMA & HDMA](https://wiki.superfamicom.org/dma-and-hdma))

## 6. CGRAM, VRAM, color math, windows

- **CGRAM:** 256 colors, **15-bit BGR** (R 0–4, G 5–9, B 10–14). **CGADD $2121** addr;
  **CGDATA $2122** write twice; **$213B** read twice. ([Fullsnes](https://problemkaputt.de/fullsnes.htm), [SNESdev PPU registers](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/PPU_registers))
- **VRAM:** **64 KiB = 32 K words**, word-addressed. **VMAIN $2115** = increment step (1/32/128
  words) + remap + high/low increment toggle. **VMADDL/H $2116/7**, write **$2118/9**, read
  **$2139/A** (prefetch-latch). Tile sizes: 2bpp=16 B, 4bpp=32 B, 8bpp=64 B per 8×8. ([SNESdev PPU registers](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/PPU_registers), [Fullsnes](https://problemkaputt.de/fullsnes.htm))
- **Color math:** **CGWSEL $2130** (window black/transparent + addend select + direct color),
  **CGADSUB $2131** (add/sub, half, per-layer enable), **COLDATA $2132** (fixed color). **Windows
  $2123–$2129** (W1/W2, OR/AND/XOR/XNOR, positions). **TM $212C / TS $212D** main/sub layer
  enable. ([SNESdev PPU registers](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/PPU_registers))

## 7. Frame structure / resolutions

- Standard **256×224**; overscan **256×239** (SETINI bit 2); forced hi-res **512** (Modes 5/6) or
  pseudo-hires (SETINI bit 3); interlace **448/478** (SETINI bit 0). **SETINI $2133** `EX.. HOiI`:
  bit0 interlace, bit1 OBJ interlace, bit2 overscan, bit3 pseudo-hires, bit6 EXTBG, bit7 ext sync.
  ([SNESdev PPU registers](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/PPU_registers), [SNESdev Backgrounds](https://snes.nesdev.org/wiki/Backgrounds))

## Implementation notes

1. **Master-clock timebase, not pure dot-lockstep** — dots are 4 clocks except 2–4 long dots/line;
   CPU is 6/8/12 clk/access; lines vary (1360/1364/1368). Model the master clock, derive dot/CPU/HDMA
   phases.
2. **HDMA fires at H≈$116, steals a content-dependent cycle count (18 + 8/24/channel + 8/byte),
   preempts GP-DMA.** Budget ≤466 clk/line.
3. **GP-DMA halts the CPU entirely** — model as a CPU stall at the MDMAEN write.
4. **5C77 (sprites/Mode7-mult/STAT77) vs 5C78 (CGRAM/output/counters/STAT78)** → two sub-modules.
5. Document the **340-vs-341 dot convention**; the invariant is 1364 = 336×4 + 4×5.

### Note on a flagged discrepancy
Sources differ on dots-per-scanline (340 vs 341) — a numbering convention, not a hardware
disagreement. Anomie/Super-Famicom-wiki count 341 dot cycles = 1364 master clocks; SNESdev counts
340 dots with two 6-clock long dots. Pick one and be consistent.
